intro: Justicia scheidweileri, also known as Mexican honeysuckle or golden dewdrop, is a flowering plant native to tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Justicia scheidweileri include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Justicia scheidweileri is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as dried flowers and leaves, which are used to make capsules and tablets for the treatment of various health conditions.
